A NWS SURVEY INDICATED AN EF1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR HIGHWAY 39 NEAR COUNTY ROAD 10500. THE PATH WAS ABOUT 2 MILES LONG AND 200 YARDS WIDE. THE TORNADO LIFTED NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF COUNTY ROAD 1201 AND 1251. HALF A ROOF WAS BLOWN OFF ON A HOUSE. SEVERAL TREES WERE UPROOTED AND DAMAGE TO OUT BUILDINGS AND OTHER STRUCTURES. WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AROUND 90 MPH. THE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR ABOUT 3 MINUTES.

ROOF AND SIDING DAMAGE AT A HOUSE. A POWER POLE SNAPPED. LARGE TREE DOWN.
HIGHWAT T HAD NUMEROUS TREES DOWN SOME STRUCTURE DAMAGE. TREES BLOCKING SIDE ROADS.

PRELIMINARY REPORT FROM SURVEY TEAM INDICATES AN EF1 TORNADO TOUCH DOWN 4 MILES NORTHEAST OF GERSTER OR 5 MILES SOUTHWEST OF QUINCY NEAR HIGHWAY TT. WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 90 TO 100 MPH. THERE WAS TREE DAMAGE AND SOME UPROOTED. TWO BARNS AND OUTBUILDINGS WERE DESTROYED. NO INFORMATION WAS AVAIABLE FOR WIDTH OR PATH LENGTH AT THIS TIME AND SURVEYS ARE STILL ONGOING.

DAMAGE OCCURRED TO SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS AND BARNS...ALONG WITH MINOR ROOF DAMAGE TO HOMES WEST OF WILLARD...JUST SOUTH OF U.S. 160. ALL DEBRIS WAS BLOWN TO THE EAST...WITH NO CONVERGENT DAMAGE SIGNATURE OBSERVED. THEREFORE...IT APPEARS THIS DAMAGE WAS THE RESULT OF AN ENHANCED AREA OF STRAIGHT LINE WINDS ALONG A BROAD...MILE LONG PATH. WIND SPEEDS ARE ESTIMATED TO HAVE BEEN AROUND 70 MPH.

AN EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ON THE WEST SIDE OF LOCKWOOD AT THE GOLF COURSE AND CITY POOL AREA. THIS TORNADO TRACKED EAST-NORTHEAST TO THE NORTH END OF SYCAMORE ST. THIS IS A DELAYED REPORT. NWS AND POLK COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT HAVE DETERMINED THAT AN EF-0 TORNADO OCCURRED DURING THE OVERNIGHT HOURS OF 5/20/13. TORNADO STARTED 1NNW BOLIVAR AROUND 102AM TRAVELED APPROXIMATELY 2 MILES AND LIFTED AROUND 108AM ABOUT 1NE BOLIVAR. ONE TRAILER WAS BLOWN ONTO A ROAD...SMALL BARN OVERTURNED...AND SEVERAL TREES WERE UPROOTED. ESTIMATED WIND SPEED WAS 85 MPH AND PATH WIDTH WAS 100 YDS.
